name: Jofaz Transportation
principals: Joseph Fazzia (owner); Fred Auseher (Manager per source data)
fleet_size_est: ~614 buses across Jofaz + 3rd Avenue Transit + Y&M Transit (combined family-owned group, three Brooklyn yards)
service_types: school bus / NYC DOE student transportation (regular and likely SPED routes)
districts_or_customers: New York City Department of Education (NYC area school children)
recent_news: 2022: NY AG Letitia James sued Jofaz, 3rd Avenue Transit and Y&M Transit (all Fazzia-owned, 614 buses) for repeated anti-idling violations near NYC schools after a prior settlement; ongoing environmental compliance issue.
fit_notes: Substantial NYC DOE family-owned platform (~614 buses across affiliated entities) - attractive scale but active AG litigation/idling-compliance overhang and NYC DOE contract complexity are material diligence risks for a Summit tuck-in.

name: Mc Means Bus Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Local Armstrong/Jefferson/Clarion County area districts (specific district not confirmed; not on current Armstrong SD contractor list)
recent_news: No recent M&A or material news identified; long-tenured small family operator in rural western PA.
fit_notes: Classic rural family micro-operator with likely succession runway given founder-era ownership; very small fleet limits standalone value but plausible bolt-on into a western PA route cluster.

name: Safe-Way Bus Co
principals: Dan, Jane and Tom Stiles (second-generation owners; founders Worth and Mary Stiles, deceased 2006/2008); Vicki Java now operates Somerset terminal after Dan Stiles' passing in 2021; 'D Stiles' in source data refers to founder/Dan Stiles legacy record
fleet_size_est: ~185 buses across 3 terminals (Twin Cities MN, Hudson WI, Somerset WI); Somerset terminal ~25 buses
service_types: school bus (regular); some charter; serves K-12 public and charter schools
districts_or_customers: St. Paul Public Schools (ISD 625), Inver Grove Heights (ISD 199), South St. Paul (Special District 6), Hudson WI public schools, Somerset WI public schools, Hope Community Academy, Laura Jeffrey Charter School, ACTC
recent_news: Founders deceased 2006/2008; Dan Stiles (Somerset manager) passed in 2021, succeeded operationally by Vicki Java - active generational/management transition.
fit_notes: High-quality second-generation MN/WI family platform (~185 buses, 25K students/day) with clear succession signal after recent owner-operator death; meaningful Twin Cities district book makes it an attractive Midwest expansion tuck-in, though geography is outside Summit's traditional footprint.

name: P R Brown Bus Inc
fleet_size_est: small (~10-16 buses implied by ~16 employees and $390K annual district contract)
service_types: school bus (regular home-to-school plus after-school activity runs)
districts_or_customers: Clarion Area School District (Clarion County, PA) - primary contract worth ~$390K for 2022-23 with CPI escalators; cameras installed on all buses
recent_news: June 2022: Clarion Area School Board unanimously approved new 3-year contract with Paul R. Brown Busing at ~$390K/year with CPI escalators; district pays fuel, $500/bus/yr camera offset.
fit_notes: Sole-owner micro-contractor anchored to one Clarion County district contract; too small to be a standalone platform but reasonable rural-PA bolt-on if Summit is consolidating routes around a regional hub.

name: Polonez Bus Svc Inc
owner_or_parent: unknown (likely Polish-American family operator based on name)
service_types: school bus; bus lines/transportation (likely some charter)
districts_or_customers: Pocono Mountains area (Scotrun / Mount Pocono); Pocono Mountain SD itself contracts with First Student, so Polonez likely serves nearby private/charter schools, regional districts, or charter work - not confirmed
recent_news: No recent M&A or material news identified.
fit_notes: Very small Pocono-area operator with thin public footprint; not confirmed as a school-district prime contractor (Pocono Mountain SD uses First Student). Limited tuck-in value absent verified contracts.
